item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My god. Now that is a scary thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Notable parallels exist between information elicited via SCP-1193-02 and demonstrable facts about SCP-1193-01."

It is "confined" doe to a "restrained limb." Its "arm" is stuck in the drainage pipe, and the rest of the body is stuck in the planet Earth.

Its "cousin" is coming "soon" to "pick it up." The arm entity will be freed. By a similar entity.

Oh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is absolutely fantastic, but I just can't jive with these two conflicting parts previously mentioned:

SCP-1193-01 is a genetically normal human arm of unusual length

and

the arm terminates in an unremarkable human hand of indeterminate gender

If we know that the arm is genetically normal, then we definitely know its gender. If the arm is genetically normal and human, then it doesn't have an indeterminate gender. Determining sex is the easiest thing to do when analyzing DNA. It is strictly easier to determine whether the hand is a boy or a girl than it is to tell if the hand is even human (genes-wise). Either the arm is genetically normal and we know it, or we don't know it's gender, it can't be both.

I don't know if this is enough of an annoyance to lose my upvote because it's a small problem but an easy fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me, I feel like this Skip is trying to tell jokes to who ever answers the phone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to downvote until I got to the interview logs at the bottom and something about them made me hang onto it…your statement here reaffirms that feeling. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s one hell of a word game there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would explain why it was so apologetic …

item.title: A Lie
item.content:encoded: quite simple ths individual was placed there, the abnormality itself its a shackle, maybe if you use DMT drug and get a better look. he is imprisoned, always ask with a different story about how he is stuck and wants help to release the arm, once he achieve this who knows what keter monstrosity would be unleashed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I KNOW I upvoted this before, this is one of my top 5 favorites, so how did I do it again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sound like a garden path sentence to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… Can we introduce this guy to 2952?

And have a Calvin and Hobbes style comic of their freindship?

Can this please exist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy the strangeness of this, the bizarre and troubling rather than the outright horrifying. I don't make the connection the author is suggesting though. In my headcanon (I think is is 6 or 7), an anomaly formed when someone was trapped in the pipe. Maybe something trapped them in the pipe, and thats where the phone came from too. Then somewhere else in the world, someone disappeared from a hospital. Then, somewhere else in the world, someone crawled into their oven, and disappeared.

They are calling for help. Every time, the arm gets longer.

item.title: I Need More!
item.content:encoded:
Echoing what others have said, this SCP is great. Just enough mystery and weirdness to grab you and hold you long after you've finished reading it.

I would love to see experiments involving interaction with the hand and arm - testing for pulse, touching, shaking, giving it objects. Especially, interaction with the hand while talking over the phone could be interesting, to see if the voice on the other end reacts in any way. I'm not even sure at this point whether the hand moves or shows signs of life, based on what the article has given me.

I would just like to see more audio transcriptions, even. This article's content is so golden, I wanted much more of it when I finished!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I demand an audio version of this with Jon Heder in the role of SCP-1193-01.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seeing your name while scrolling through recent forum posts gave me a mini heart attack. Just a few more months and everyone's least favorite nerd will be back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not back from my hiatus (I'm still punching Putin's dog) but I'm almost people again and therefore have internet access, so I want to take this brief opportunity to link you all to this. It seems relevant.

item.title: Re: Alternate interpretation...
item.content:encoded: That's a very interesting interpretation. This is the reason why I love this skip; there are so many ways to interpret and understand it, and every single interpretation is absolutely horrifying in some way.

item.title: Alternate interpretation...
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea of #4, that this would make perfect sense if we had any fucking clue what is actually going on in the universe as a whole, and that 1193-1's frustration with us is perfectly reasonable.

But the longer I think about the thing itself, the more I lean toward somewhere else, much closer to the world we know.

Here's my theory...
Have you ever worked tech support?

There was an entity, somewhere, that was planning to infiltrate human society. It researched us and prepared itself to travel from its point of origin to Earth, assume the guise of a normal human being, and accomplish some goal.
After it set out on this expedition, something went wrong, and now it's stuck. It can't go forward to Earth, and it can't go backward to wherever it came from. It almost certainly can't communicate with its point of origin. (It may be physically located within the Earth's mantle or core; it may be in some other spatial dimension or plane of reality that we can't perceive or access.)
It did manage to extrude some appendage into the vicinity of its destination (like a human trapped behind a locked door who punches through the window), probably hoping to open a wider passage and escape, or to retrieve some resource from this side that could be useful. This didn't work- in the locked door analogy, the window is too far from the doorknob to reach over and unlock the door (or no doorknob exists), and the window is too small to simply climb through. But it can reach this side in some capacity, once it does some gymnastics like boring a hole up through Earth's mantle.
So it can't get out by itself, and it can't get help from home. It's concluded that the best of its limited options is to get help from humans. It reached out in our direction again, this time with some appendage or device for communication purposes, and then used the disguise capability from the original plan to present the extension as something capable of passing communications humans could understand and would accept- a telephone. To prepare for this tactic, it also disguised the first thing it reached out with as something that would indicate the nature of its situation to humans and avoid creating too much fuss- a trapped hand, to indicate a trapped sapient being. So now our world contains a phone that connects to the entity, and a hand that's attached to it.
Now it's got a working line of communication to humanity, and it's using it. Its requests for help are based on its observations of humans during the planning stages, combined with what it knows about what's happened to it. It's not literally describing what's going on, in the same way that the arm clearly isn't an actual human arm- it's using an analogy, trying to get us to either understand what's going on and how to help, or understand that we know less than it does and should take its instructions.
Some aspects of its situation translate pretty easily to its observations, and so it mimics the behaviors of humans in similar situations- things like asking for someone in authority when you need help, repeating yourself angrily when someone who should understand your meaning doesn't, and claiming you'll find another way when you need help but you're not getting it. (It's also possible that the "cousin" references are a valid translation/analogy- there is at least one more of these things that knows where this one was going and will eventually show up and expect to find it. That would explain why "cousin" is consistent from conversation to conversation, when the explanation of the problem isn't.) But other aspects are hard or impossible for it to translate- either there are human equivalents and it missed them in its observations, or the equivalents just don't exist. Trying to translate them produces things like "bottom arm" or "went into the stove to get some cakes," which humans don't get at all. Maybe it says these things because it doesn't know they're gibberish, or maybe it says them for the same reason it talks to humans in the first place- because it can't find any better options.

TL;DR: SCP-1193 is an American tourist trapped in its hotel room in a non-English-speaking country, using a shitty phrasebook to ask for help and getting mad when no one understands.

Still upvoted, because it's nice and freaky in a way I haven't seen before. And even after my interpretation, the phone calls are still hilarious with a side of creepy. (What is it apologizing for at the end of the second one? What happens when its "cousin" gets here?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is brilliant. A solid +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually went looking - doesn't look like there's a formal one, but people tend to cite 1981 and 1000 when debunking the "curse".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a list somewhere?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Arright, but it needs to be added to the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I like ophite as an author and as much as I like this, I have to say that Ronald Reagan Cut Up While Talking has this beat. Bigfoot, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one, right here? This needs to be the go-to reference for "There is NO FIRST SCP CURSE!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ophite, you are one of my favorite writers on the site. After I read this, I was really confused. And then I thought abput it more, and it slowly but surely all hit me. Wow….just….simply wow. No words can do this justice, but I hope a +1 will suffice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Three years later, yoric encountered the single best post on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Oh wow. Now I am really glad I checked the discussion to see if the writer explained more of it, because I knew I was missing something.

item.title: A few months later...
item.content:encoded:
…but for a good cause: my very first upvote!

This is the very first SCP I read, before I knew what the site was about. No need to tell you that "….wat…?!?!?!?!?!' accurately describes how I felt at first…

Excellent. I love the humour, the strangeness, the nonsensical fridge horror… Everything! Especially this:

Communications via SCP-1193-02, mostly concerning SCP-1193-01's oven and refrigerator, indicate that SCP-1193-01 may lack conventional thermoreception

This is just too good!

item.title: Re: +300
item.content:encoded: About damn time, too.
Yeyeyeyeyeyey

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wat.

But the good kind. Still, though.

Wat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep reading this up and down, and even read Ophite's post, and I just still don't get it. It just seems to me to be a buried giant making prank calls. That's all I keep seeing, like a colorblind person trying to see a hidden image in a color-dotted picture

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. Weird and great.

My interpretation is that it is an entity living (possibly trapped) deep inside the planet that is trying to imitate a human being by analyzing human speech patterns, trying to figure out what they mean, and then mimicking them. The arm is also probably an attempt at imitating a human being. However, because it is so completely different than humanity and has probably never directly observed a human being, it has a hard time comprehending what it hears and therefore its imitation isn't that great.

I don't think anything is actually changing on the other end. It's just trying to come up with different metaphors to describe its current predicament (possibly the predicament of being stuck inside a planet).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is not just one of the most creative things I've ever read, it's an absolute master class in story writing. There is not one unnecessary word in the whole thing. It builds incredible tension, not just between characters in the story but between the story and the reader herself. It seems to be written with absolutely certainty about what it is trying to say and thus it never has to say it outright. It comes in, shows just enough and no more, and then it's out. Fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always loved this one, because of the way it delivers the horror. Instead of scaring us via vivid descriptions and explanations, chopping the SCP into bits and showing us how each little part works and why it does this and how it achieves that, it shows us this thing… and doesn't explain it at all. Doing this lets your mind run amok with possibilities, each thousands of times creepier/more unsettling than the last. Personally, I like to imagine that the Earth is just some weird contraption that the Buried Giant's race created, and that his cousin is some sort of engineer-type guy who will dismantle our Earth when he arrives, so that the Giant can free his hand. But I have no fucking clue, and the mystery and ambiguity of this SCP is why I love it so much. +1'd with the force of a thousand suns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having read the article multiple times in the span of a year or two, and having read the comments by the author and other users, I can only think of one thing that hasn't been pointed out yet: We don't know how thick the arm is. "Genetically normal" and "unremarkable" certainly imply that it's the same thickness as you or I's arm. However, relying on inferences alone isn't something I'm keen on doing. Is the arm only a few centimeters wide, or is it somewhat more proportional to its length? Obviously not anywhere near a 1:1 ratio or whatever it would be for the average arm, but, y'know… Thicker'n ours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I was reading another one of ophite's SCPs and came here and what immediately drew me in was that this SCP lives where I do. Welp. I wanna find this phone and try to give poor David some advice. (Or write a tale about the Class-1 Personnel who visits his parents and decides to talk to this fellow.)

One question for the author: was the guy a soldier? Sounds like he'd be an asset to the…

…arm-y!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to talk in depth about the description because of how much i love this:

SCP-1193-01 is a genetically normal human

zzzz

arm

Whoa, what? (This is, in a sense, a garden path sentence.)

of unusual length

How unusual? Must be a couple meters or something.

located in a drainage pipe inside the basement of a telephone switching station in Scottsdale, Arizona.

Whoa, wait, what? Is it just laying there? Note that location is not blacked out, as it is not needed to be blacked out, it is mundane.

Ten centimeters below the drainage grate, the arm terminates in an unremarkable human hand of indeterminate gender.

Okay. Ten centimeters, that is not very unusual at all.

Upon initial examination, the arm was believed to extend through the drainage pipe to a depth of 35 meters.

Wait a second, what?

Subsequent endoscopic investigation demonstrated that the arm extends to at least a depth of 71 kilometers, passing without apparent difficulty through the Mohorovičić discontinuity and the upper mantle, with regularly spaced elbows every four kilometers. Elbows below a depth of 26 kilometers are slightly retroflexed to accommodate a nine-degree southward bend in the drainage borehole. Continued endoscopic investigation into the SCP-1193-01 borehole is suspended due to presently-intractable engineering constraints.

The escalation is ridiculous here. 71 kilometers and the usage of "Mohorovičić discontinuity" and "regularly spaced elbows every four kilometers" in the same sentence. At this moment it far exceeds any viewer's reasonable expectations, this is achieved by a simple understatement in the first paragraph that is written in a way to specifically set the expectations low.

And now it switches back to completely mundane, discussing reasonable engineering concerns, in a futile attempt to distract the viewer from the 71+ km long arm.

This paragraph is also a short version of the entire article, as it entirely consists of escalation: turns out, the arm is the most normal thing in the whole debacle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of people have brought up "The Moving Finger" (which I haven't read), but what it reminded me of was Lovecraft's "The Shunned House", specifically a scene toward the end where the protagonist digs under the basement of the house and finds a bizarre object that he eventually realizes is an impossibly huge elbow.

item.title: Re: Problem
item.content:encoded: Beyond what Bob stated, I will also add that the results of a karyotype aren't actually as useful and concrete in sexing an individual as you suggest. There are conditions that cause XY individuals to express a (somewhat anomalous) female phenotype, and vice versa. And that's even assuming the same rules apply to a tens-of-kilometers-long arm with multiple regularly spaced elbows at all, "genetically normal" or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just from reading it, I was leaning toward 3 or a less concrete or developed version of 4 — that there was something about all this we were simply failing to understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing parts of the human body used to make things horrifying is pretty much par for the course.

Things of immense size and unnatural proportions are kind of par for the course when invoking cosmic dread.

Mysterious telephone calls are common.

Somehow, the combination of all three is the most unique thing I've read in ages, and invoked that sense of cosmic horror I haven't felt since I first read Lovecraft all those years ago with not a tentacle in sight. Bravo, sir! I regret only I have but one upvote to give.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
Ah! Great to see you're still around! This and the intestine one have been in my head all day. I can't stop thinking about how creepy/weird/mundane they are.

Thank you so much for all your products.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: I'm still around. I just don't have as much time now as I did while I was clerking, and have to ration my writing for lunches and breaks. I do have sketches for three more SCPs and two Tales, but have been focusing on a big nonfiction project.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: This is my favorite SCP. It's too bad the author is no longer around. But he posted a new one in May. At some point he mentioned writing a work of fiction with this SCP and the similar intestinal one, I hope he gets around to it.

A bit late but congrats! Too bad the author isn't hanging around anymore; he seemed a brilliant man, able to put into words what the rest of us can barely comprehend. I think he knows a thing or two about the universe, and someday we might just find him setting up a telephone line in a basement so that we can talk to a giant arm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is a güt one. I like it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always interpreted this as: (5) The entity communicating to us via this is stuck somehow, most likely in between this and another reality / dimension (terms used loosely). It's trying to communicate its predicament to the Foundation, but it operates on such a different level of thinking that this is how it translates: it thinks in general relationships between objects in the same way you'd think about trying to relate a message in a language you poorly understand. It might as well tell us it's caught in event horizon of a black hole, or being sat on by an elephant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes I wonder if we have reality all fucking wrong.

+1 with the force of a million suns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you hand the phone to the arm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well shit. I guess I've been looking at this all wrong…The only way I ever interpreted this SCP was that normal people from all around the world would get their hands stuck in something and make calls to someone they know, but the calls would somehow get routed to this phone.

Crap, I don't even get how I was supposed to come to any of those four conclusions. I feel kind of stupid now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the casual implication of a world operating under very different rules than our own. I wish I could write like this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The part that really gets me the most, is the log, in which someone's asking for help, and gets this weird Dr. Iqbal on the line, whose answers are contradictory and who won't help a fellow out with a simple problem. It's such a beautiful reversal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix'd, and at least is a good pet peeve =P

item.title: Re: Solution?
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I was proposing a solution, not informing you that there already was a solution. I should have made that more explicit.

item.title: Re: Solution?
item.content:encoded: then the description should mention that.

item.title: Solution?
item.content:encoded: Arm is chimeric and contains cells which are both XX and XY. (Which is a thing that can happen in real life.)

item.title: Problem
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-1193-01 is a genetically normal human arm"

and

"the arm terminates in an unremarkable human hand of indeterminate gender"

If the arm is "genetically normal", then we've taken tissue samples from it and analyzed the DNA of the cells therein.

And if we've done that, then we can damn well ascertain its gender. Just look if it's got a Y chromosome, or two X's.
Because fuck logic. I feel like I should attach a picture of a hat wearing a dog to this.
